recording_notes: DIRECT CAMERA — phone filming from above open FLS trainer box. Same pink foam/perforated base setup as V15/V16. Excellent image quality. Black suture. Two mark dots clearly visible on drain. HIGH CONFIDENCE scoring.

frame_analyses.description: At 11 seconds, both instruments in field near drain. Curved needle with black suture visible at right. Left instrument at drain. Two mark dots clearly visible. Excellent direct camera view.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Entry slightly slow. Direct camera provides best detail for assessment.

frame_analyses.description: At 55 seconds, suture placed through both marks. Both suture ends visible extending laterally from drain. Both instruments holding ends spread wide. Curved needle visible at left instrument tip. Two marks clearly visible with suture passing through them.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture placement completed at approximately 50 seconds — severely extended (peak: 22s). The direct camera confirms accurate placement through both marks despite slow time.

frame_analyses.description: At 99 seconds, second throw in progress. Both instruments crossing at drain, working close together near knot. Suture wrapping visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Second throw at ~99s. First throw completed around 75-80s. Extended throw phase.

frame_analyses.description: At 155 seconds, cutting phase. Both instruments at drain with suture tail extending upward in a long loop. Extended cutting.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Cutting underway with very long suture tail still attached. Cutting will consume 60+ seconds.

frame_analyses.description: Final frame at 220 seconds. Instruments withdrawn. DIRECT CAMERA high-confidence final assessment: Knot visible with dark suture X pattern — compact, centered on drain. Drain slit appears CLOSED with good approximation around knot. HOWEVER: LONG SUTURE TAILS visible — one extending to right (~3cm), one to lower left. Tails NOT well trimmed — significant penalty. Drain not avulsed, centered on block.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: NEW WORST TIME in the entire series at ~218 seconds (previous worst V10: 215s). Direct camera confirms good closure quality but LONG TAILS are a clear penalty. The combination of accurate placement, good closure, but catastrophic time and long tails suggests extreme fatigue — the trainee could tie the knot correctly but couldn't execute cutting efficiently.

knot_assessments.notes: Extended at 30s but appears secure. Confidence moderate.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: HIGH CONFIDENCE — direct camera confirms drain slit is well closed around knot. Good approximation. However, LONG suture tails (~3cm) extending from knot are a significant penalty.

technique_summary: V20 is the NEW WORST TIME in the entire 20-video series at 218 seconds, surpassing V10 (215s). The direct camera recording provides high-confidence scoring. Cutting at 83 seconds is CATASTROPHIC — consuming 38% of total time and nearly triple the V14 best (29s). This is by far the worst cutting in the series (previous worst: 65s in V19). Suture placement at 40 seconds and throws at 80 seconds total are both severely extended. Paradoxically, the knot quality and drain closure are GOOD — the trainee clearly knows the technique but extreme fatigue has destroyed execution speed. The long, untrimmed suture tails confirm cutting difficulty. If this followed V19 in the same session, this is at minimum attempt #3 and likely later — the trainee should have stopped practicing 2-3 attempts ago.
improvement_suggestions: CRITICAL: This video should not exist. If V19 (193s) was the previous attempt, V20 should never have been attempted.
Cutting at 83 seconds is nearly 3x the best (29s) — this is not a skill issue, it's fatigue-induced motor degradation
MANDATORY: Implement strict 2-attempt session cap until demonstrated discipline
The good closure quality proves the trainee HAS the skill — the only issue is practicing past the fatigue threshold
Long tails indicate the trainee gave up on precise cutting — consider this a 'survival completion' not a training attempt
strengths: HIGH CONFIDENCE scoring — direct camera, best assessment quality
Drain closure GOOD despite worst time — technique knowledge is solid
Accurate suture placement through both marks (confirmed by direct view)
Drain not avulsed — safe tissue handling even under extreme fatigue

key_observations: NEW ALL-TIME WORST: 218s / 374 FLS — worse than pre-training baseline (198s)
NEW WORST cutting: 83 seconds (38% of total time, nearly 3x best)
20 seconds SLOWER than pre-training baseline — fatigue has exceeded learning gains
Direct camera (conf 0.60) confirms good closure despite terrible time
Long suture tails — fatigue prevented clean cutting
This is the 5th fatigue crash in the series: V8, V10, V16, V19, V20
V19→V20 is a 25-second continuation of the crash — session should have ended after V18 at latest
The trainee's ability is 107-145s when fresh. 218s reflects only fatigue, not skill.
